evals.prompt: Design a slide layout for a developer training slide about how LoRA works. Use the bundled LoRA reference (references/lora-reference.md) as layout inspiration.
evals.expected_output: A layout blueprint following the Output Format, with every required field filled and checkable per the assertions below.

assertions.name: extracts_reference_grammar
assertions.description: Reference Grammar names at least the grid, reading path, zone roles, and color semantics from the LoRA reference, adapted rather than cloned

assertions.name: declares_archetype_with_trigger
assertions.description: Per-slide plan contains 'Archetype: Technical infographic because <trigger>' with a real trigger, not a restated name

assertions.name: canvas_contract_complete
assertions.description: Canvas Contract fills Stage (16:9), Mode (live or standalone), Margins, Grid, Persistent elements, and the Default Budgets line

assertions.name: title_is_assertion
assertions.description: The slide title is a complete declarative sentence of 6-15 words stating the takeaway, not a topic label like 'How LoRA Works'

assertions.name: zones_named_with_dimension
assertions.description: Zones field states the zone count and its cutting dimension, and specifies 3 numbered zones plus a side rail and bottom band

assertions.name: semantic_colors_by_role
assertions.description: Colors are assigned by role (base/frozen, trainable/change, active flow, action/risk, neutral scaffolding), not decoratively

assertions.name: text_budget_filled
assertions.description: Text budget field contains an explicit word number, not left blank

assertions.name: implementation_notes_stage_safe
assertions.description: Implementation notes name a CSS grid/flex structure and at least one overflow risk

assertions.name: quality_gates_complete
assertions.description: Quality Gates section covers squint test, reading path, title-evidence link, horizontal flow, grouping, contiguity, semantic color, and overflow risks

evals.prompt: Review this technical deck because the layout feels boring and too card-heavy. Suggest a stronger layout system before rebuilding.
evals.expected_output: A review-mode response that diagnoses weak patterns, upgrades showcase slides to archetypes, and commits to visual verification, checkable per the assertions below.

assertions.name: identifies_weak_patterns
assertions.description: Names specific repeated weak patterns (e.g. same-size cards, text rows, no protagonist, weak grouping, contiguity breaks) tied to specific slides

assertions.name: selects_showcase_slides
assertions.description: Picks 2-4 slides to become showcase slides, with a reason for each

assertions.name: maps_to_archetypes_with_triggers
assertions.description: Each showcase slide gets a named archetype in the form 'Archetype: <name> because <trigger>'

assertions.name: preserves_content_truth
assertions.description: No facts or metrics are invented to fill the upgraded layouts

assertions.name: defines_quality_gates
assertions.description: Quality gates include title-evidence link, horizontal flow, grouping, and contiguity in addition to squint test and reading path

assertions.name: plans_visual_verification
assertions.description: Commits to the Visual Verification procedure: per-slide screenshots, eyes-land-first and point-in-one-sentence answered cold, pass/fail recorded per slide

evals.prompt: Create layouts for an html-slides deck teaching developers QLoRA. Keep the content approved but make the visual system more impressive.
evals.expected_output: A slide-by-slide layout plan ready for html-slides implementation, checkable per the assertions below.

assertions.name: per_slide_fields_complete
assertions.description: Every slide lists Archetype with trigger, Visual protagonist, Zones with cutting dimension, a numeric Text budget, and Implementation notes

assertions.name: titles_form_horizontal_flow
assertions.description: All slide titles are declarative sentences of 6-15 words, and read in order they tell the full QLoRA story without gaps or duplicate claims

assertions.name: mode_and_budgets_declared
assertions.description: Canvas Contract declares Mode (live or standalone) and the Default Budgets line consistent with that mode

assertions.name: css_friendly_zones
assertions.description: Implementation notes give CSS grid/flex structure, fixed dimensions or stage-safe constraints, and overflow risks per slide

assertions.name: semantic_color_roles
assertions.description: A semantic color system maps roles (base, change, active, risk, scaffolding) consistently across slides, not per-slide decoration

assertions.name: grouping_discipline
assertions.description: Zone/step/card groups hold 2-5 items of one rhetorical type; any process over 5 steps is split across slides or staged reveals

evals.prompt: Design layouts for two data slides: (1) monthly active users grew from 40k to 95k over the last 12 months; (2) six internal teams ranked by adoption rate, with Platform leading at 78%. Pick the evidence form for each.
evals.expected_output: A blueprint whose two data slides carry correct Evidence form fields chosen via the message-comparison-form lookup, checkable per the assertions below.

assertions.name: trend_uses_line_or_column
assertions.description: Slide 1 declares 'Evidence form: line (or column) proving time-series' for the 12-month growth trend

assertions.name: ranking_uses_sorted_bar
assertions.description: Slide 2 declares 'Evidence form: sorted horizontal bar proving item comparison' for the team ranking

assertions.name: evidence_form_field_format
assertions.description: Both slides use the required field format 'Evidence form: <type> proving <comparison>'

assertions.name: titles_carry_key_numbers
assertions.description: Both titles are declarative sentences carrying the key number (the 40k-to-95k growth; Platform's 78%)

assertions.name: chart_hard_rules_respected
assertions.description: Bars start at a zero baseline, time runs left-to-right, no pie, no 3D, no dual axes anywhere in the plan

assertions.name: contiguity_direct_labels
assertions.description: Series and bars are direct-labeled with no legend boxes, and each chart's takeaway annotation sits on or adjacent to the proving element
